service_description: Grocery-style food pantry for South Brunswick residents in need. Registration required — call or email to sign up.
eligibility: South Brunswick residents only. Proof of address and income required.

org_name: Islamic Society of Central Jersey Food Pantry
service_description: Food boxes including pasta, rice, canned goods, bread, frozen chicken, and toiletries. Open alternating Saturdays — call or email to confirm.

org_name: Hands of Hope for the Community
service_description: Supplemental groceries, personal care, baby care, and household items. Clients must apply and interview. Bring own reusable bags.
eligibility: Residents of Cranbury Dayton Kendall Park Kingston Monmouth Junction and Plainsboro

service_description: Emergency and temporary lodging, meals, physical and mental health assessments, crisis counseling, and employment assistance for homeless men.
eligibility: Homeless men in Middlesex County. Must agree to refrain from alcohol and drugs and abide by shelter rules.

service_description: 24-bed emergency shelter for domestic violence survivors and their children. Multilingual case workers, food pantry, clothing, linens, and school supplies provided.
eligibility: Women and children fleeing domestic violence — Middlesex County

service_description: Coordinated entry for shelter and permanent housing. Case management, emergency motel placement when no shelter is available, and diversion assistance.
eligibility: Middlesex County residents who are homeless or at imminent risk

service_description: Volunteer stewardship including trail work, invasive species removal, litter cleanups, native plant restoration, and river cleanups throughout county parks.
eligibility: Open to all volunteers age 16 and older
hours: Saturdays spring and fall plus Mon-Fri summers

service_description: Water quality monitoring (StreamWatch), river and lake sampling, native plant restoration, and conservation education events including World Water Day and Butterfly Festival.
eligibility: Volunteers age 14 and older with adult; age 16 solo

service_description: Refugee resettlement case management, affordable housing placement, English language learning (ELL), cultural integration, and connections to mental health and healthcare services.
eligibility: Refugees asylees and other ORR-eligible immigrants in Central and Southern NJ
address: 19 S Second Ave Highland Park NJ 08904

org_name: Jewish Family Services of Middlesex County
service_description: Social services counseling and advocacy including immigration and citizenship services mental health support and case management for individuals and families.
eligibility: Middlesex County residents regardless of religion or background

service_description: Immigration legal services and orientation social services counseling emergency assistance and community outreach for low-income and vulnerable residents of Middlesex County.
eligibility: Low-income and vulnerable residents of Middlesex County

service_description: Free 24/7 multilingual helpline connecting callers to over 6600 community programs for food utilities housing mental health substance use childcare transportation and more.
